'''Jack Buchanan''' (born 10 April 1992) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Burleigh Bears]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Playing career==
Born in [[Culburra Beach]], New South Wales, Australia, Buchanan played his earliest football for the Culburra Dolphins before joining Canterbury's representative teams as a teenager. "I was doing 2½-hour one-way trips to the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Bulldogs]] in [[Harold Matthews Cup|Harold Matthews]] and [[S. G. Ball Cup|SG Ball]]. Five hours all round, about five days a week. That would have been about three years," Buchanan later said.<ref>{{cite news|author=Glenn Jackson|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=He's done the miles, now Buchanan's journey has reached the big league|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/hes-done-the-miles-now-buchanans-journey-has-reached-the-big-league-20130314-2g3id.html|accessdate=18 April 2013|date=15 March 2013}}</ref>

Buchanan was later signed by the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. He played for the Dragons' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2011 and 2012, scoring 3 tries in 46 games.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/b/buchanan_jack/index.php|accessdate=11 March 2013|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120624231536/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/b/buchanan_jack/index.php|title=rleague.com – Buchanan, Jack|archivedate=24 June 2012 }}</ref> In 2012, he was statistically one of the competition's top ten players in hit-ups, metres gained, and tackles made. At the end of the season, he signed a 3-year contract with the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news|work=weststigers.com.au|title=Top young NYC prop signs for three years|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Top-young-NYC-prop-signs-for-three-years/63786|accessdate=17 April 2013|date=11 September 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130520052214/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Top-young-NYC-prop-signs-for-three-years/63786|archive-date=20 May 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In Round 1 of the [[2013 NRL season]] Buchanan made his [[NRL]] debut for the Tigers against the [[Newcastle Knights]]. He was the youngest starting prop in the competition.<ref>{{cite news|author=Glenn Jackson|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Young guns prepared to prop up Tigers|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/young-guns-prepared-to-prop-up-tigers-20130325-2gqbj.html|accessdate=18 April 2013|date=26 March 2013}}</ref> With injuries to props [[Keith Galloway]] and [[Aaron Woods]], Buchanan missed only one game throughout the season, averaging 26 tackles per game. He was also one of the club leaders in support runs.<ref name="young gun">{{cite web|author=Brock Corfe|work=weststigers.com.au|title=Young guns – Jack Buchanan|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Young-guns--Jack-Buchanan/82642|accessdate=15 September 2013|date=22 August 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029183621/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Young-guns--Jack-Buchanan/82642|archive-date=29 October 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref> Coach [[Mick Potter]] said of Buchanan's first year, "When Jack came across here, he really was a big kid that was still a bit skinny. Getting some weight on him was key. He probably won’t be fully developed for three more years but he is certainly one to keep your eye on."<ref name="young gun" />

With the arrival of props [[Martin Taupau]] and [[James Gavet]], Buchanan made just 4 appearances from the bench in 2014.

Buchanan played in 12 games in 2015, again all as a reserve. At the end of the season, he re-signed for a further year.<ref>{{cite news| publisher= ABC News|location=Australia| title=Wests Tigers sign Ipswich Jets' Billy McConnachie, Rod Griffin; re-sign prop Jack Buchanan for 2016 season| url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-10-13/tigers-snap-up-pair-of-ipswich-jets-lock-in-jack-buchanan/6851290|accessdate=18 December 2015|date=13 October 2015}}</ref>

On 11 May 2016, Buchanan joined the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]] effective immediately on a contract to the end of 2017, after being released from his Tigers contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article/49587/widnes-secure-nrl-prop-forward|title=Widnes secure NRL Prop Forward|publisher=Widnesvikings.co.uk|accessdate=2016-05-11|archive-date=5 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160605084549/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/article/49587/widnes-secure-nrl-prop-forward|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2009, Buchanan played for the New South Wales U17's team.
